name: usaddress (US Address Parser)

performance.notes: CRF-based; trained model loaded at import; fast per-address

agent_readiness.notes: Parse and tag US addresses using CRF. import usaddress. usaddress.parse('123 Main St NE Minneapolis MN 55413'). Returns [(token, label)] tuples with labels: AddressNumber, StreetName, StreetNamePostType, PlaceName, StateName, ZipCode, etc. usaddress.tag('123 Main St NE Minneapolis MN 55413'). Returns {'AddressNumber': '123', 'StreetName': 'Main', ...}, 'Street Address'. Handles: PO Box, corner addresses, rural routes, apartment numbers. Raises usaddress.RepeatedLabelError on ambiguous input.

gotchas: US addresses only — no international support (use libpostal for global)
RepeatedLabelError raised for ambiguous addresses — must handle exception
PO Box addresses need special handling
Model hasn't been updated recently — some edge cases fail
best_when: Parsing and normalizing US addresses from free-text
Extracting street number, name, city, state, ZIP from combined strings
Pre-processing addresses for geocoding or deduplication
avoid_when: High-accuracy address standardization (use USPS CASS certification services)
summary: usaddress parses US addresses using CRF. usaddress.tag('123 Main St NE Minneapolis MN 55413') → {'AddressNumber': '123', 'StreetName': 'Main', ...}. parse() returns tagged tokens. Handles apartments, PO Box, rural routes. MIT by DataMade. US-only. Handle RepeatedLabelError for ambiguous input. Good for free-text US address parsing before geocoding or deduplication.
